title: Core Philosophy
heading: Core Philosophy
description: The architectural principles that drive the design of pw-core.

principles.title: Single Source of Truth
principles.desc: URLs, selectors, and testIds live in one config object.

principles.title: Readability
principles.desc: Tests should read like logical business workflows, not selector hunts.

principles.title: Scalability
principles.desc: Adding new pages or views should not increase overall framework complexity.

principles.title: Reusability
principles.desc: Components (such as grids and sidebars) are defined once and reused everywhere.

principles.title: Type Safety
principles.desc: Catch element name typos and mistakes during development instead of runtime.

principles.title: AI-Friendly
principles.desc: Standardised structural patterns significantly reduce context size and token usage.

why.title: Why pw-core?
why.description: Most Playwright test suites start small and manageable. However, as the codebase grows, it often runs into critical challenges:
negatives: Massive Page Objects containing verbose locators
Inconsistent coding styles and locator conventions
